mappings.rationaleAI: The nervous system covers nerve cells, synaptic transmission and neurotransmitters, Galvani's bioelectricity experiments, and the distinction between sensory and motor pathways -- directly addressing bioelectricity, neuron structure, nerve impulse nature, and synaptic transmission.

Cell biology covers cell membrane structure and function, organelle compartmentalization, and the distinction between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, directly addressing the nature of membranes and cell compartmentalization.
Lipids as membrane components (the lipid bilayer) directly relate to the nature of cell membranes and their composition from macromolecular constituents.
Mitochondria as membrane-bound organelles illustrate compartmentalization of the cell, with their double-membrane structure enabling ATP synthesis.
Circadian rhythms involve molecular feedback loops and the suprachiasmatic nucleus processing neural signals, relevant to the nature of nerve impulses and their role in biological timing.
Water's properties including osmotic behavior and its role as the universal solvent directly relate to the movement of water across cell membranes through osmosis.
